title: Standardize revision-2 review to plain-language contract, shield revision-1 append-only pilot
context: EXP-03 revision-1 was onboarding/UX pilot only. Revision-2 needs structured, accessible review that requires no domain policy knowledge and works consistently across all arms.
decision: Revision-2 cases must state required_relationship in plain language. All arms then present identical question ('Does rule preserve relationship?') with 4 standardized choices (use/correct/reject/cannot_decide) and simple response template. Revision-1 cases with no required_relationship keep byte-identical treatments to preserve append-only pilot auditability.
consequences: Reviewer UX shifts from domain-specific complexity to 4 plain-language outcomes
All arms receive identical review structure in revision-2 (vs arm-specific before)
required_relationship is mandatory for revision-2, optional for revision-1
Revision-1 pilot data remains replayable under original hash; comparison between versions requires explicit revision field
alternatives_rejected: Mandate required_relationship in revision-1 (breaks append-only pilot invariant)
Keep revision-1 question per arm but add relationship field (doesn't achieve simplification goal)
Open-ended response format (loses consistency for aggregation)

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Mandate required_relationship in revision-1 (breaks append-only pilot invariant)
Keep revision-1 question per arm but add relationship field (doesn't achieve simplification goal)
Open-ended response format (loses consistency for aggregation)
